name: POSTINFLAMMATORY POLYPOSIS
definition: PSEUDOPOLYPOSIS reepithelialized inflammatory polyps as sequelae of mucosal ulceration

demographics.Etiology: ulcerative colitis (10-20%); granulomatous colitis (less frequent); schistosomiasis
demographics.Location: most common in left hemicolon, may occur in stomach / small intestine

distribution: most common in left hemicolon, may occur in stomach / small intestine
discriminating_features: filiform polyposis = multiple wormlike projections only attached at their bases

general: filiform polyposis = multiple wormlike projections only attached at their bases
